channel.title: ☕️General - Frontity Community Forum
channel.description: Topics in the '☕️General' category Want to say something but don’t know where? Try here!

item.title: How to Contact SBCGlobal Email Support: Troubleshooting and Assistance
item.dc:creator: eddismcdonald
item.description:
Get live expert help with your SBCGLOBAL needs—from email and passwords, technical questions, mobile email and more. Call Live SBCGLOBAL Support at 1-844-604-3244.

In the digital age, email has become an indispensable part of our communication. However, encountering issues with your email service, such as SBCGlobal, can be frustrating. Whether it’s login problems, configuration issues, or email delivery troubles, having access to reliable support is crucial. This guide aims to provide a detailed overview of various methods to contact SBCGlobal email support for prompt assistance.

If you need to contact Sbcglobal Email Support, dial or 1-(844)-604-3244, Help Center, Email, Chat Support.

1. AT&T Support Website:

AT&T, the parent company handling SBCGlobal services, offers comprehensive support through its website. To access SBCGlobal email support:

  • Visit the AT&T Mail support for AT&T Mail customers website
  • Navigate to the Email section or use the search function to find specific SBCGlobal email-related topics.
  • Explore a wealth of resources including FAQs, troubleshooting guides, and articles addressing common issues.

2. Live Chat Support:

One of the quickest ways to get assistance is through live chat support:

  • On the AT&T support website, look for the live chat option.
  • Initiate a chat session and connect with a support representative in real-time.
  • Describe your SBCGlobal email issue, and the representative will guide you through troubleshooting steps or provide solutions.

3. Phone Support:

AT&T offers phone support for users requiring personalized assistance:

  • Contact AT&T support through their dedicated helpline. The phone number +1-(844)-604-3244 might vary based on location and the nature of the issue.
  • Dial the SBCglobal customer service number and follow the prompts to connect with the department handling SBCGlobal email support.
  • Have your account details ready, and describe the issue concisely to the support representative.

4. Community Forums:

Engage in AT&T’s community forums for peer-to-peer support:

  • Access the forums on the AT&T support website where SBCGlobal email users interact.
  • Browse existing threads or post your queries to seek guidance from other users who might have faced similar issues.
  • Moderators or experienced community members often provide valuable solutions and tips.

5. Social Media Channels:

Some companies offer support through social media platforms:

  • Check if AT&T has official profiles on platforms like Twitter or Facebook.
  • Look for specific handles dedicated to support and send direct messages detailing your SBCGlobal email issue.
  • Social media support might offer a different avenue for assistance and can sometimes provide swift resolutions.

6. AT&T Stores:

If accessible, consider visiting a local AT&T store for in-person assistance:

  • Store representatives might provide guidance regarding SBCGlobal email issues.
  • They can advise on support channels or offer basic troubleshooting steps.

Tips for Contacting Support:

  • Gather essential account information like your SBCGlobal email address, associated phone number, or account details before contacting support.
  • Describe the issue clearly and provide relevant details such as error messages, device information, or recent changes that might have triggered the problem.
  • Patience is key. Support representatives will work diligently to resolve your issue, but some complex issues might take time to resolve.

Understanding Common Issues:

  1. Login Problems: Users may face difficulties logging into their SBCGlobal email accounts due to forgotten passwords, account lockouts, or server-related issues.
  2. Configuration Errors: Setting up SBCGlobal email on various devices (phones, tablets, or email clients) might lead to configuration errors causing issues with sending or receiving emails.
  3. Email Delivery Problems: Users might experience delays or failures in sending or receiving emails, which can stem from server issues or incorrect settings.
  4. Security Concerns: Account security, spam, or phishing emails can pose risks to SBCGlobal email users, requiring proper measures to safeguard their accounts.

In conclusion,

To contact SBCGlobal Email Support, the fastest way is to dial. After that, you will be able to talk to a live SBCGlobal Email customer support representative. Having access to reliable support channels is crucial when facing SBCGlobal email problems. Utilize the various contact methods provided by AT&T to seek assistance, and ensure to provide accurate information to expedite the troubleshooting process. By leveraging these support options, users can resolve issues swiftly and continue enjoying their SBCGlobal email experience hassle-free.

FAQS -

Is There a Way to Contact Sbcglobal Email Service via Phone?

Yes, you can reach the Sbcglobal customer support representative by dialing 844-604-3244 number. It’s the Sbcglobal tech support phone number. So, dial this number and get appropriate guidelines, tips, advice, or instructions from the agent to solve your problem.

How do I contact Sbcglobal Email directly for support?

To contact Sbcglobal directly for support, dial to talk to a live Sbcglobal support agent. In addition, you can contact Sbcglobal via chat, email, contact forms, Report a Problem, and social media platforms.

How Do I Get Back Into My Sbcglobal Email Account?

If you can`t access your SBCGlobal email account, you can visit AT&T Mail support for AT&T Mail customers website & commence a chat session with support representative or call +1-(844)-604-3244 to speak to a live SBCGlobal customer service agent. They will give you simple guidelines, instructions, on how you can recover and get your old SBCGlobal email account back.

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: How to Contact SBCGlobal Email Support: Troubleshooting and Assistance

item.title: How do I contact SBCglobal customer service? | United States
item.dc:creator: lindaburt007
item.description:
• The best way to contact SBCglobal’s customer service team is through the AT&T Mail support for AT&T Mail customers website.
  • If you’ve been the victim of an hacked SBCglobal email account, you can call their assistance team at 1-844-604-3244.
  • AT&T’s website also has dozens of articles that offer solutions to common issues you might face with your Sbcglobal email account.

How to reach the SBCGlobal customer support?

If you are looking to contact SBCglobal customer service, there are several ways to do so. Here are some options:

1) AT&T Mail Support: You can visit the official AT&T Mail support page and browse through the topics to find helpful information. You can also explore the video library to learn more about your SBCglobal email account services.

2) Phone Support: Call the SBCglobal customer service through their toll-free number 1-844-604-3244 for troubleshooting support. They are available from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM PT, 7 days a week to assist you with any complicated issues you may have with your email account.

3) Live Chat Support: This is another medium to get in touch with SBCglobal email support by visiting the AT&T Contact Us page, then tap on “Chat Available” and choose which AT&T service you’d like to live chat about button. The live chat is available every day from 7 a.m.-1 a.m ET.

4) Community Forums: You can also get in touch with SBCglobal tech support via forums, and communities to get answers for your questions with SBCglobal email account.

We hope this helps! Let us know if you have any other questions.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

How do I recover my SBCGlobal email account?
Visit the AT&T Sign-In Page: Go to the AT&T sign-in page since SBCglobal.net email services are now under AT&T’s umbrella. Click “Forgot Password”: Look for the “Forgot Password” link below the login fields and click on it. Enter Your User ID: Provide your SBCglobal email address or User ID. Follow the on-screen instructions to verify your identity.

Why is my SBCglobal email not working?
Here are some of the most common causes:

a) Internet connection problems: If you don’t have an internet connection, you won’t be able to access your SBCGlobal email. Make sure that your internet is turned on and that you are connected to a network.
b) Server problems: There may be a problem with the SBCglobal email servers. If this is the case, you will need to wait for the issue to be resolved.
c) Incorrect email settings: If your email settings are incorrect, you may not be able to send or receive emails. Check your email settings to make sure that they are correct.

How do I log into my SBCGlobal.net email account?
You’ll need to use AT&T’s sign in page in order to access your SBCGlobal.net email account (+1-844-604-3244). There isn’t a separate page for SBCGlobal users - all domains now owned by AT&T use the same sign in page.

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: How do I contact SBCglobal customer service? | United States

item.title: Potential upcoming issue with Vercel
item.dc:creator: brett
item.description:
Hi there!

I received a notification from Vercel a couple months ago saying that they were disabling Node.js 16 in February 2024. From what I understand, I believe Frontity only works up until version 16.

Is this looming issue or is there a solution in place? Getting kind of nervous and wondering what the options are for our website. Thanks in advance!

7 posts - 4 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Potential upcoming issue with Vercel

item.title: Deployment
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi there,

I have my Wordpress site in a subfolder inside Azure server and I’m about to deploy the react version to the root of the domain.
Azure runs nodejs and I would like to know if my site will run just uploading the files from the build folder or I have to do something else to make it work.

Kind regards,

Fernando

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Deployment

item.title: How to load a local page
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi there,

I’m wondering if I can load a local page instead of fetching from wordpress.

As it’s not possible to fetch everything from wordpress, I would like to create my own page in react and display it.

If that is possible, how would that can be accomplished?

Kind regards,

Fernando

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: How to load a local page

item.title: Set language to html tag
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi there,

Does anyone ever changed the html language tag?

If so, how can we all achieve this feature?

Kind regards,

Fernando

3 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Set language to html tag

item.title: How to render an accordion from Wordpress?
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi All,

I would like to know if anyone had an experience to have to render an accordion from Wordpress in Frontity React.

Any idea how to achieve it would be appreciated.

Kind regards,

Fernando Fas

3 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: How to render an accordion from Wordpress?

item.title: If we could do Frontity again
item.dc:creator: rickey29
item.description:
If we could turn the time back, if we could do the Frontity again from the beginning, what should we continue to do, what should we do it differently? Thanks!

3 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: If we could do Frontity again

item.title: How i can add the “lang” attribute to the rendered html tag?
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Does anyone know how to add the lang on the html tag?

Thank you

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic

item.title: How to deploy the files in the build folder to azure?
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi there,

Just wondering if anyone has deployed the files from the build folder to azure server.

If yes, please guide me.

Thank you in advance.

Fernando

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic

item.title: Can I just deploy the files in the build folder to my host?
item.dc:creator: fernandofas
item.description:
Hi there,

I’m not new neither expert with Frontity, but just would like to know if I can just get the files from the build folder and place inside my webspace in a host and that’s it.

If not, and someone can give me the directions. i really appreciate.

Kind regards,

Fernando

3 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Can I just deploy the files in the build folder to my host?

item.title: Agency use cases for React + WordPress?
item.dc:creator: mikes
item.description:
Hi guys,

I wonder if in an agency scenario, how can frontity help with cutting costs, improving maintenance and security, etc? I can understand that GitHub’s CI/CD can be very helpful especially with React. And that Storybook is useful for documenting and testing components.

Are there other scenarios where Frontity should be the go-to for agencies to use it?

PS I’m speaking in terms of increasing revenue for companies.

Thanks!

  • Mike

2 posts - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Agency use cases for React + WordPress?

You can get all the computer components at reasonable prices at our online store harddiskdirect

item.title: WARNING: All your builds using @frontity/now will break as of August 9th 2022
item.dc:creator: ni.bonev
item.description:
Just a heads up to fellow Frontity users who are using Vercel to deploy their sites.

As of August 9th 2022, Vercel is deprecating node v12.x.
The current builder that Frontity uses for deploying to Vercel, GitHub - frontity/now-builder: A Frontity builder for the Vercel hosting, is basically fixed to v12.x

We are currently looking for a solution for adjusting this, but just wanted to give a heads-up to the fellow frontity users that might have not heard about this. Once we have found a solution it will be shared here.

20 posts - 8 participants

Read full topic
item.source: WARNING: All your builds using @frontity/now will break as of August 9th 2022

item.title: Partytown and Frontity Vendors
item.dc:creator: francis
item.description:
Partytown is in beta now and the idea of using workers is pretty neat to move off the main thread to improve performance.

Has anyone tried using party town on their Frontity implementations?

GitHub

GitHub - BuilderIO/partytown: Relocate resource intensive third-party scripts...

Relocate resource intensive third-party scripts off of the main thread and into a web worker. 🎉 - GitHub - BuilderIO/partytown: Relocate resource intensive third-party scripts off of the main threa...

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Partytown and Frontity Vendors

item.title: Why nodejs in frontity
item.dc:creator: vibethemes
item.description:
So, I want to understand why we are using nodejs in frontitiy. ReactJS is perfectly capable of sending API calls and capturing the JSON data to render HTML.
So why is there a need to add a nodejs server in between ?

5 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Why nodejs in frontity

item.title: Using Frontity within other frameworks
item.dc:creator: thewizardofwicacy
item.description:
I was wondering if anyone has explored using frontity within other frameworks, such as react native and react native for web. Also wondering if a containerized use-case could be achieved. I am working on an adaptive-card-like framework for embedding instant books with actionable content and navigation into existing apps and sites via sdk, and need to create a solution for wordpress. Any thoughts?

2 posts - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: Using Frontity within other frameworks

item.title: Full page editing (WordPress 5.8)
item.dc:creator: 403page
item.description:
Yo yo!

With WordPress getting super close to full site editing with Gutenberg… they have added in some really interesting sitewide blocks (like site logo, etc). They also added support for a theme.json that will contain things like global colors.

I’m thinking about making a plugin that added endpoints for all these new blocks (and the ones on the roadmap, like headers and footers).

Wouldn’t it be super cool for non-devs to be able to update these components in the wordpress backend with gutenberg and theyll reflect automatically on their frontity site without needing to rebuild it?

I think so. Interested in any thoughts you fine folks might have.

3 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Full page editing (WordPress 5.8)

item.title: 🌱 Frontity Community Forum Users Guide
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Welcome to the Frontity Community Forum!

Frontity is an Open Source Project with a growing community. This Community Forum is the main place for the community to make announcements, engage in conversations with one another, and help each other. The support provided in this forum is especially important because it will allow other Frontity users to benefit from the answers provided.

This guide will help you to understand how the Forum is organized and how to use it. This guide will also set some rules to encourage nice, friendly and productive interactions among the Community members.

The Frontity Community Forum works around the following key ideas:

  • It’s based in Discourse
  • It’s organized in Categories
  • It’s a good place to Get Help
  • It has a Community Netiquette

Based in Discourse

Discourse is the platform used by this forum.
In their own words

Discourse is the 100% open source discussion platform built for the next decade of the Internet. Use it as a mailing list, discussion forum, long-form chat room, and more!

To understand how this Community Forum works and how you can make the most when reading topics and taking part in conversations we recommend you to read the following post (all the explanations provided there can be applied in this forum):

Discourse Meta – 10 Apr 21

Discourse New User Guide

Welcome! As a new user of Discourse, we hope you will find this site intuitive and clearly structured, but here is some guidance to get you started: Basic Terms Used by Discourse (Nomenclature) Here are some very basic terms that will be helpful to...

Reading time: 17 mins 🕑 Likes: 150 ❤

Organized in categories

The Forum is organized around the following categories:

  • Announcements
    General news and announcements.

  • 🙋 Get Help
    The “Get Help” category is the place for asking generic technical questions about Frontity or about how to solve a specific issue you’re having in your Frontity project. You can contribute to the project by providing support to these current unanswered questions.

  • Framework Development
    In this category you’ll find the latest updates about the work done (releases, sprints…) for the framework and its ecosystem (packages & themes).

  • Feature Discussions
    Suggest new features to keep improving Frontity, vote the ones you are interested in, follow the progress and join the discussion of their design.

  • Community
    Have you created a Frontity site, written a post about the framework, or organized a Frontity meetup? Whatever your idea is, feel free to share it here!

  • Jobs
    Are you open to Frontity freelance projects? Do you need a freelancer to work on a custom Frontity project? This is the place to post about it.

  • General
    Want to say something but don’t know where? Try here!

To Get Help

The most common use of this forum is to ask for help for a specific project.

Before asking in the Community Forum

Before asking in the Community Forum we recommend you to check the Frontity documentation and the following learning resources.

Community Forum

Use the forum advanced search option to make sure this question has not already been asked and answered by other users.

Documentation

  • docs.frontity.org - Introduction, concepts and guides
  • api.frontity.org - API Reference
  • tutorial.frontity.org - Step by Step guide

Blog

In Frontity’s Blog, there are some technical posts that go deep into some topics. Some of the most relevant ones are:

  • SEO for Headless WordPress Themes
  • How to Create a React WordPress Theme in 30 Minutes
  • Connecting Gutenberg and Frontity

Videos

  • YouTube Frontity Channel - The Frontity Talks and Frontity Demos are especially interesting to learn about Frontity.

Demos

You have several demos available compiled in the repository GitHub - frontity-demos/frontity-examples: A monorepo consisting of a number of different projects each of which is intended to demonstrate how to achieve a particular task using Frontity.

Asking questions in the Community Forum

If you have an issue with your Frontity project (that you’re not able to solve by checking the Frontity documentation), take into account that there may be a variety of reasons for the issue - some of which may not be directly Frontity-related - so it’s very important to provide as much information as you can when describing an issue in a specific project and to provide the resources so the issue can be replicated by anyone.

In this post, you’ll find a guide with suggestions of the info to provide when asking about “how to solve a specific issue with your Frontity project”

About the 🙋Get Help category 🙋Get Help
Ask about “how to solve a specific issue with your Frontity project” If you want to ask about “how to solve an issue with your Frontity project” we suggest you to provide things such as: ✓ Description of your issue ✓ System info → npx frontity info ✓ Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal ✓ Specific errors you’re getting in the browser’s console ✓ A repository or codesandbox with the code of your project ✓ A deployed version of your site ✓ The URL of your WP REST API The more inf…

The more info you provide about your issue the better. Providing a repo or code-sandbox with your code is especially helpful to enable members of the community to find solutions to your technical or code-related issues. Having a publicly accessible API is also useful.

When asking for help with a technical problem that you are encountering please bear in mind the following two points:

  1. have I provided enough information for anyone to be able to understand the problem that I am having? For example:

    • error messages in the terminal
    • error messages in the browser console
    • steps to reproduce the problem

  2. have I provided the resources needed so that anyone who could help me is able to reproduce the issue in their machine? For example:

    • link to a repo
    • codesandbox
    • publicly accessible API

Providing comprehensive information about your problem is the quickest and best way to get useful answers from other members of the Community that will help you to resolve your issue.

Community Netiquette

The term “netiquette” is a contraction of the words Internet and Etiquette. It is used to define the set of rules and guidelines for maintaining a pleasant, supportive, and nurturing environment for communication, as well as to avoid the possibility of conflict among users.

The 10 Netiquette rules for this Frontity Community Forum are:

  1. Search Before Posting. Before posting a question or asking for help on a particular topic, use the forum search option to make sure this question has not already been asked and answered by other users.

  2. Check the Documentation Before Posting. Before posting a question or asking for help on a particular topic, check the documentation and learning resources mentioned above. There’s a good chance that you will find answers to your questions there.

  3. Don’t Double Post. If you create a post and no one replies, then leave it at that. Reposting the same message or slightly edited versions of the same message will not earn you any respect from other forum users and it is considered to be annoying by the community.

  4. Be Polite and Use a Friendly Tone. Expressions like “Please”, “Thanks in advance” or “Any help would be appreciated” will make your question look good and will help you to get the quickest answer from the Community.

  5. Respect Other People’s Time. Keep your online communication succinct and to the point, be accurate in your explanations, be clear in your questions and provide all the information and resources needed so that other Community members can fully understand and reproduce your issue. Avoid vagueness, lack of clarity, and making people “guess” what you’re trying to say: this will make it easier to get an answer to your post from other Community members.

  6. Do Not Expect a Reply Immediately. You will probably get an answer to your question, but it may not happen quickly. Please don’t post a question on the forums, and then an hour later, post a reply that says “anyone?” and an hour later another that says “so nobody knows the answer?” and so on. “Bumping” your issue comes across as petulant and inconsiderate. Be patient. People may be involved in other important stuff. When posting a question take the following into account:

    • Frontity is a global Open Source project used by people worldwide, so community members are distributed across different timezones
    • Contributors of the framework may not be able to attend to questions in the Forum due to other priorities
    • Community members volunteer their help and provide support using their own valuable time.

  7. Write in English. English is the official language of the Frontity Community Forum. Frontity is used worldwide and the community members and contributors of the framework are distributed across several countries so the official language in this forum is English because:

    • Most of the people that may be able to help you with your issue (Framework contributors and Community members) use English as their main language
    • Maybe another community member (English speaker) has had a similar issue and can help you with your problem or add more relevant information
    • Once the problem is solved, another community member may benefit from the solution and will be able to follow the discussion.

  8. Share your Knowledge. You can easily give back to the Frontity Community and support your fellow Frontity users by sharing your existing knowledge with less experienced users, answering questions, or sharing your point of view about the topic. When doing so:

    • maintain a friendly tone,
    • don’t try to assert your “superiority” because you know something that they don’t
    • ensure that any technical information you provide is clear and, above all, accurate!
    • remember that you also started somewhere.

  9. Stay on Topic. Don’t post irrelevant comments in a thread. Start a new topic if your comment is not directly related to the topic of the thread. Don’t “hijack” other people’s threads for your own ends.

  10. Be Supportive and Inclusive. Community members come from a variety of backgrounds and have differing levels of technical knowledge. Don’t assert your “superiority” in any way and always strive to help those who have less knowledge than you. Never forget: by giving your knowledge away you don’t have any less of it (unlike cake ).

1 post - 1 participant

Read full topic
item.source: 🌱 Frontity Community Forum Users Guide

item.title: Unable to log into the Forum in Firefox via Google Account
item.dc:creator: david1
item.description:
As the title says, when I click “Log In” on the latest version of Firefox in Pop!_OS (Ubuntu) 20.10, I get a “Login Error”. The only way around it is to open a private window and log in from there. Before, the login was working perfectly on my computers (I’ve now tried on two). Also, I noticed sometimes logging in via the private window will log me in on the non-private one.

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Unable to log into the Forum in Firefox via Google Account

item.title: Struggling with working remote for the first time? This can help
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
Last week, our CEO & Co-founder @Pablo gave an online session to help those teams who are struggling with working remote for the first time during this COVID-19 outbreak. He talked about our remote work-dynamic and shared best practices on being a more productive remote team.

If you are interested in learning more, here are the slides of the presentation. You can also watch the session in Spanish here.

Additional guides:

  • Internal meetings best practices
  • Slack etiquette & tips

Please feel free to share this with teams who need the support.

Don’t hesitate to reach out with any ask questions. We’ll do our best to help in any way we can.

3 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Struggling with working remote for the first time? This can help

item.title: [JS for WP Conference] Free workshop for potential speakers
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
This year, the JavaScript for WordPress team is increasing their efforts to make the JavaScript for WordPress conference as diverse and inclusive as possible. They are seeking more people of color, LGBTQ+, people who entered tech later in their careers, neurodivergent people, those with different physical abilities, and folks who come from underrepresented parts of the world.

To encourage new speakers to come forward, they are holding a free workshop where you can get inspiration, motivation, and direction on planning and submitting a talk to the 2020 conference.

The workshop will be held Saturday, March 14, 2020. Registration is still open.

If you’re interested, you can find more information here: https://javascriptforwp.com/workshop-announcement-2020/.

P.S. If you would like to submit a talk on Frontity to the JS for WP Conference, let us know. We’ll be happy to help as well!

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: [JS for WP Conference] Free workshop for potential speakers

item.title: WordCamp US 2019
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
Hey there!

@luisherranz and I will be attending WordCamp US this year. If any of you will be there we would love to meet!

Ps. We will bring Frontity t-shirts and stickers to gift

7 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: WordCamp US 2019

item.title: How can we make Frontity better?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Hi everyone !

We are in a continuous process of improving the framework and we would like to do it focused on your needs.

We’d love to understand how do you usually operate, what problems do you find and check together how Frontity could ease your work. This could be pretty useful in order to develop new features or release new documentation to meet your needs.

We’d be delighted to have a quick chat about this with anyone interested, as we really believe listening to your feedback is the best way to keep improving.

Would you have 15-20 mins to talk? Feel free to select any slot on my calendar, or send me a message if you don’t find a suitable date.

We could also use this talk to help you with any questions or problems you may have with Frontity, or even advise you on any other topics related if needed.

Thanks for your help!

3 posts - 3 participants

Read full topic
item.source: How can we make Frontity better?

item.title: Podcast about the Automattic/Tumblr acquisition
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
Hi! I just wanted to share this podcast from Wordfence about the Automattic/Tumblr acquisition.

They go into how this may advance headless WordPress and the challenge of integrating the tech, among many other things. I found it quite interesting.

Wordfence – 13 Aug 19

Podcast Episode 38: Automattic Buys Tumblr from Verizon

The Wall Street Journal reported on Monday, August 12, 2019 that Verizon is selling social media and blogging platform Tumblr to Automattic for an undisclosed sum, though rumors state that it may be as low as $3 million dollars. After the...

2 posts - 2 participants

Read full topic
item.source: Podcast about the Automattic/Tumblr acquisition
